page.title: Cabopino Family Day: Boat Rental for Snorkeling and Beaches

data.meta_description: Book a Cabopino family snorkeling boat rental from Marbella. Explore hidden coves, clear waters, and sandy beaches with skipper, drinks, and snacks included.
data.summary: Discover the best family-friendly boat rental for snorkeling and beaches near Cabopino. Our fleet departs Puerto Banús and includes licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, and snacks for a stress-free day on the Costa del Sol.
key_facts: All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish VAT (21%).
Free soft drinks, water, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks on board.
Cabopino beach is a 20–30 minute cruise from Puerto Banús (approx. 5 NM).
Snorkeling gear available on request; bring your own masks for best fit.
Family-friendly boats like Astondoa 40 (9 pax) and Azimut 39 (11 pax) from €749/2h.
Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) includes a free jet ski; minimum 4h at €4,719.
Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés are calm snorkeling spots near Cabopino.
No fixed itineraries – you choose the pace and stops with your skipper.

faq.q: How long does it take to reach Cabopino from Puerto Banús by boat?
faq.a: From Puerto Banús, Cabopino is roughly 5 nautical miles east along the coast. At cruising speed (18–22 knots on most of our boats), you’ll arrive in 20–30 minutes. That leaves plenty of time for snorkeling, beach stops, and a relaxed lunch.

faq.q: Which boat is best for a family with young children?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are excellent choices. Both have spacious cockpits, stable hulls, and shaded areas. They start at €749 for 2 hours including skipper, drinks, and snacks. For larger families, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offers even more deck space and a free jet ski.

faq.q: Is snorkeling gear provided on the boat?
faq.a: We can arrange snorkeling equipment on request, but we recommend bringing your own masks and fins for the best fit and comfort. The clear waters off Cabopino, especially around Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés, are ideal for spotting fish and marine life.

faq.q: Can we stop at Cabopino beach for lunch or a swim?
faq.a: Absolutely. Cabopino beach has a protected dune system and calm waters, perfect for swimming. You can anchor offshore and paddle in, or your skipper can drop you at the beach’s wooden walkway. There are chiringuitos (beach bars) like Chiringuito Cabopino for food and drinks.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and Spanish VAT (21%). There are no hidden fees. Just bring sunscreen, towels, and your own snorkel mask if you prefer.

faq.q: Are there any age restrictions for children on board?
faq.a: No age restrictions – children of all ages are welcome. Life jackets in various sizes are available on all boats. The skipper will give a short safety briefing before departure. Families with toddlers often choose the Azimut 39 for its stable ride and ample shade.

faq.q: Can we combine Cabopino with other nearby spots?
faq.a: Yes. A popular family route is Puerto Banús → Cabopino beach (snorkel & swim) → Cala del Faro (quiet cove) → Marbella Marina (lunch) → back to Banús. Your skipper can tailor the itinerary based on weather, sea conditions, and your preferences.

faq.q: How do I book a Cabopino family snorkeling boat rental?
faq.a: Check our fleet page for available boats and use the WhatsApp link to confirm dates and pricing. For boats not listed with set rates (e.g., Fairline Targa 12m or Ferretti 94), we’ll provide a custom quote within minutes. Early booking is recommended during July and August.

data.body_html:
If you’re after a Cabopino family snorkeling boat rental that combines calm coves, sandy beaches, and zero hassle, you’ve found it. Departing from Puerto Banús, our fleet takes you east to Cabopino’s protected dunes and clear Mediterranean waters in under 30 minutes. Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance – so you can focus on the fun, not the logistics.

Why Cabopino Works for Families

Cabopino beach sits about 5 nautical miles east of Puerto Banús, tucked behind a pine-backed dune system that keeps the sea calm and the swimming safe. The seabed shelves gently, making it ideal for children learning to snorkel. Unlike the busier Marbella beaches, Cabopino feels quieter midweek, and the nearby coves – Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés – offer even more sheltered spots for fins and masks.

Our Family-Friendly Boats

We have boats to suit groups from 5 to 12 passengers. For a small family, the Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) or Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are perfect. Both start at €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for a full day (8h), including skipper, drinks, and snacks. If you want more deck space and a free jet ski, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) is available from €4,719 for a minimum 4 hours. All our boats depart from Puerto Banús and are maintained to high standards.

What’s Included – No Surprises

Every charter comes with: licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and Spanish IVA (21%). You won’t pay extra for ice, cups, or basic snorkel gear (on request). We recommend bringing your own masks for comfort, but we can supply equipment if you let us know in advance.

Sample Itinerary: 4-Hour Family Morning

  • 10:00 – Board at Puerto Banús, safety briefing, cast off.
  • 10:30 – Cruise east along the coast (20 mins to Cabopino).
  • 11:00 – Anchor off Cabopino beach or Cala del Faro. Snorkel, swim, paddle.
  • 12:30 – Light lunch on board (snacks + drinks) or beach stop at Chiringuito Cabopino.
  • 13:30 – Short cruise back to Banús, passing Marbella Marina.
  • 14:00 – Disembark in Puerto Banús.

Longer charters can add a visit to Estepona or Sotogrande, or simply more time floating in the coves.

Snorkeling Spots Near Cabopino

The best snorkeling is found at Cala del Faro (rocky outcrops with fish) and Cala Cortés (sandy bottom, clear water). Both are within 5 minutes of Cabopino by boat. The water visibility often exceeds 15 metres in summer, and you’ll see bream, wrasse, and occasional octopus. No strong currents – safe for children with life vests.

All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT. No booking fees.

How to Book Your Cabopino Family Snorkeling Boat Rental

Browse our full fleet and choose your preferred boat. Then send us a WhatsApp message with your date, group size, and preferred duration. We’ll confirm availability and send a payment link. For boats like the Ferretti 94 or Fairline Targa, we’ll provide a custom quote within minutes. Early booking is recommended for July and August weekends.

Frequently asked questions

How long does it take to reach Cabopino from Puerto Banús by boat?

From Puerto Banús, Cabopino is roughly 5 nautical miles east along the coast. At cruising speed (18–22 knots on most of our boats), you’ll arrive in 20–30 minutes. That leaves plenty of time for snorkeling, beach stops, and a relaxed lunch.

Which boat is best for a family with young children?

The Astondoa 40 (12.5m, 9 pax) and Azimut 39 (12.5m, 11 pax) are excellent choices. Both have spacious cockpits, stable hulls, and shaded areas. They start at €749 for 2 hours including skipper, drinks, and snacks. For larger families, the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) offers even more deck space and a free jet ski.

Is snorkeling gear provided on the boat?

We can arrange snorkeling equipment on request, but we recommend bringing your own masks and fins for the best fit and comfort. The clear waters off Cabopino, especially around Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés, are ideal for spotting fish and marine life.

Can we stop at Cabopino beach for lunch or a swim?

Absolutely. Cabopino beach has a protected dune system and calm waters, perfect for swimming. You can anchor offshore and paddle in, or your skipper can drop you at the beach’s wooden walkway. There are chiringuitos (beach bars) like Chiringuito Cabopino for food and drinks.

What is included in the charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, and Spanish VAT (21%). There are no hidden fees. Just bring sunscreen, towels, and your own snorkel mask if you prefer.

Are there any age restrictions for children on board?

No age restrictions – children of all ages are welcome. Life jackets in various sizes are available on all boats. The skipper will give a short safety briefing before departure. Families with toddlers often choose the Azimut 39 for its stable ride and ample shade.

Can we combine Cabopino with other nearby spots?

Yes. A popular family route is Puerto Banús → Cabopino beach (snorkel & swim) → Cala del Faro (quiet cove) → Marbella Marina (lunch) → back to Banús. Your skipper can tailor the itinerary based on weather, sea conditions, and your preferences.

How do I book a Cabopino family snorkeling boat rental?

Check our fleet page for available boats and use the WhatsApp link to confirm dates and pricing. For boats not listed with set rates (e.g., Fairline Targa 12m or Ferretti 94), we’ll provide a custom quote within minutes. Early booking is recommended during July and August.
